info.title: Clerk Platform API
info.description:
The Clerk REST Platform API, for programmatic management of resources of a workspace.

> [!note]
> This API is currently in private beta. If you'd like access for your workspace, please fill out and submit our [request form](https://clerkdev.notion.site/2df2b9ab44fe803f9031e9f3185c5e19).

tags.name: Applications
tags.description: An object representing a Clerk application. Each `application` can have multiple `instances`, typically one for development and one for production, each with distinct user pools.

tags.name: Domains
tags.description: An object representing a domain associated with a Clerk application. In development `instances`, a domain is provided by Clerk. Production `instances` require additional configuration to be set up.

tags.name: Application Transfers
tags.description:
An object representing a transfer request for an application between workspaces.

The high-level flow for application transfer:

1. The initial workspace can create an application transfer. A single-use `code` will be generated which may be distributed to any end-user of Clerk.
2. The code must be claimed through the clerk dashboard: https://dashboard.clerk.com/apps/transfer?code=<...>

Note that once a transfer is claimed, the original workspace will no longer have access to the application.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Operations for managing users within an application instance.

tags.name: JWT Templates
tags.description: Operations for managing JWT templates within an application instance.

tags.name: Redirect URLs
tags.description: Operations for managing redirect URLs within an application instance.

tags.name: Config
tags.description: Operations for managing instance configuration. The config API provides a unified interface for reading and updating instance settings, including authentication methods, SSO connections, and other instance-level configuration options.

parameters.name: include_secret_keys
parameters.description: Whether to include secret keys in the response.

parameters.name: include_secret_keys
parameters.description: Whether to include secret keys in the response. If 'true', the response will include the secret keys for each instance.

post.description: Set or replace an application's logo by uploading an image file.
Set or replace an application's favicon by uploading an image file.

patch.description: Update the production domain for an application.

get.description: List all domains for an application's production instance.

post.description: Create a provider domain for an application's production instance.

get.description: Get domain information for an application.

delete.description:
Delete a provider domain from an application's production instance.

Only provider domains can be deleted. The primary domain cannot be deleted.

get.description: Get the status of a domain for an application.

post.description:
Triggers a DNS check for a domain and returns the current domain status.

This endpoint ensures that at most one DNS check job is in-flight at any time.
If a check is already running or was recently performed, a 409 Conflict is returned.

Use this endpoint to trigger a new DNS verification check after configuring DNS records.

200.description: DNS check triggered and domain status returned successfully.

get.description:
List all transfer requests created by the authenticated workspace. Returns
transfers sorted by creation date in descending order (most recent first).

Use the `status` parameter to filter by transfer status.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
Filter by transfer status. Multiple values can be provided by repeating
the parameter (e.g., `?status=pending&status=canceled`).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page (1-500, default 10).

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description:
Cursor for pagination. Provide the ID of the last transfer from the
previous page to get the next page of results.

parameters.name: ending_before
parameters.description:
Cursor for pagination. Provide the ID of the first transfer from the
previous page to get the previous page of results.

post.description:
Create a new transfer request for an application. This initiates the process of
transferring ownership of the application to another workspace.

Only one pending transfer can exist for an application at a time. The transfer
will expire after 24 hours if not completed.

get.description: Retrieve details of an application transfer by its ID.

delete.description: Cancel an existing application transfer. Only transfers in 'pending' status can be canceled.

get.description:
Get usage totals for an application instance across meters such as MAU, MAO,
SMS, custom domains, and enterprise connections.

The `envOrInsID` parameter can be either an environment type (e.g., "development", "production")
or an instance ID.

Requires the `application_usage:read` scope.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description:
Start of the date range for usage data in YYYY-MM-DD format.
Both `start` and `end` must be provided together.
When omitted, the current billing cycle is used.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description:
Inclusive end of the date range for usage data in YYYY-MM-DD format.
Both `start` and `end` must be provided together.
When omitted, the current billing cycle is used.

get.description:
List all users for an application instance.

The `envOrInsID` parameter can be either an environment type (e.g., "development", "production")
or an instance ID.

Use the `query` parameter to filter users by searching across email addresses, phone numbers,
usernames, web3 wallets, user IDs, first names, and last names.

Use the `order_by` parameter to sort the results. Prefix with `+` for ascending or `-` for descending order.
Valid fields: `created_at`, `updated_at`, `last_sign_in_at`, `last_active_at`, `email_address`, `first_name`,
`last_name`, `username`, `phone_number`, `web3_wallet`. Default is `-created_at` (most recent first).

Requires the `users:read` scope.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
A search query to filter users. Searches across email addresses, phone numbers,
usernames, web3 wallets, user IDs, first names, and last names.

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description:
Field to order results by. Prefix with `+` for ascending or `-` for descending order.
Valid fields: `created_at`, `updated_at`, `last_sign_in_at`, `last_active_at`, `email_address`,
`first_name`, `last_name`, `username`, `phone_number`, `web3_wallet`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page (1-500, default 10).

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.

post.description:
Marks a user as banned within an application instance. This terminates their active sessions
(marks them as revoked) and prevents them from signing in again.

Requires the `users:manage` scope.

Removes the ban from a user within an application instance. This allows them to sign in again.

Requires the `users:manage` scope.

get.description:
List all JWT templates for an application instance.

Requires the `jwt_templates:read` scope.

post.description:
Create a new JWT template for an application instance.

Requires the `jwt_templates:manage` scope.

claims.description: JWT template claims in JSON format

allowed_clock_skew.description: JWT allowed clock skew in seconds

custom_signing_key.description: Whether a custom signing key/algorithm is also provided for this template

signing_algorithm.description: The custom signing algorithm to use when minting JWTs. Required if `custom_signing_key` is `true`.

signing_key.description: The custom signing private key to use when minting JWTs. Required if `custom_signing_key` is `true`.

get.description:
Retrieve a specific JWT template for an application instance.

Requires the `jwt_templates:read` scope.

patch.description:
Update an existing JWT template for an application instance.

Requires the `jwt_templates:manage` scope.

claims.description: JWT template claims in JSON format

allowed_clock_skew.description: JWT allowed clock skew in seconds

custom_signing_key.description: Whether a custom signing key/algorithm is also provided for this template

signing_algorithm.description: The custom signing algorithm to use when minting JWTs. Required if `custom_signing_key` is `true`.

signing_key.description: The custom signing private key to use when minting JWTs. Required if `custom_signing_key` is `true`.

delete.description:
Delete a JWT template from an application instance.

Requires the `jwt_templates:manage` scope.

get.description:
List all redirect URLs for an application instance.

The `envOrInsID` parameter can be either an environment type (e.g., "development", "production")
or an instance ID.

Requires the `applications:read` scope.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page (1-500, default 10).

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.

post.description:
Create a redirect URL for an application instance.

The `envOrInsID` parameter can be either an environment type (e.g., "development", "production")
or an instance ID.

Requires the `applications:manage` scope.

url.description: The full URL value prefixed with `https://` or a custom scheme e.g. `"https://my-app.com/oauth-callback"` or `"my-app://oauth-callback"`.

get.description:
Retrieve a redirect URL for an application instance.

The `envOrInsID` parameter can be either an environment type (e.g., "development", "production")
or an instance ID.

Requires the `applications:read` scope.

delete.description:
Delete a redirect URL from an application instance.

The `envOrInsID` parameter can be either an environment type (e.g., "development", "production")
or an instance ID.

Requires the `applications:manage` scope.

get.description:
Get the configuration for an application instance.

The `envOrInsID` parameter can be either an environment type (e.g., "development", "production")
or an instance ID.

Optionally filter to specific config keys using the `keys` query parameter.

Requires the `applications:manage` scope.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: Config keys to retrieve. If not specified, all keys are returned.

patch.description:
Update the configuration for an application instance.

The `envOrInsID` parameter can be either an environment type (e.g., "development", "production")
or an instance ID.

Use the `dry_run` query parameter to preview changes without applying them.
Use the `destructive` query parameter to allow clearing config keys by setting them to null.
Use the `If-Match` header to provide optimistic concurrency control via config version.

Requires the `applications:manage` scope.

parameters.name: dry_run
parameters.description: If true, preview the changes without applying them.

parameters.name: destructive
parameters.description: If true, allow clearing config keys by setting them to null.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: Config keys to return in the response. If not specified, only updated keys are returned.

parameters.name: If-Match
parameters.description: Config version for optimistic concurrency control.

get.description:
Get the JSON Schema for the instance configuration.

The `envOrInsID` parameter can be either an environment type (e.g., "development", "production")
or an instance ID.

Optionally filter to specific config keys using the `keys` query parameter.

Requires the `applications:manage` scope.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: Config keys to retrieve schema for. If not specified, all keys are returned.

PlatformApplicationInstance.title: Application Instance
PlatformApplicationInstance.description: Represents an instance (environment) within a Clerk application. Each application can have multiple instances, typically one for development and one for production.

instance_id.description: The unique identifier for this instance. Use this ID when making API calls that target a specific instance.

environment_type.description: The environment type of the instance. Determines whether this instance is used for development/testing or production workloads.

secret_key.description: The secret key for authenticating server-side API requests to this instance. Keep this value secure and never expose it in client-side code.

publishable_key.description: The publishable key for this instance. Safe to include in client-side code and used to initialize Clerk SDKs in the browser.

instances.description: List of instances associated with this application.

proxy_path.description: Custom proxy path for provider domains (e.g., "/__clerk"). Must start with a leading slash and be a single path segment. Defaults to "/__clerk" if not provided.

environment_types.description: List of environment types to create instances for. Accepted values are "development" and "production". Defaults to "development" if not provided.

template.description: Application template to use for configuring the application (e.g., "b2b-saas", "b2c-saas", "waitlist").

id.description: The ID of the deleted resource.

proxy_path.description: Optional proxy path for provider domains.

is_satellite.description: Whether this is a satellite domain.

is_provider_domain.description: Whether this is a provider domain.

PlatformListApplicationDomainsResponse.title: List Application Domains Response
PlatformListApplicationDomainsResponse.description: A paginated list of application domains.

total_count.description: The total number of application domains for the application.

proxy_path.description: Optional proxy path for provider domains.

PlatformDNSCheckResponse.description:
Response from triggering a DNS check. Includes the current domain status
along with metadata about the check.

last_run_at.description:
The timestamp in epoch milliseconds of the last DNS check, if any.
Will be `null` if no check has been performed yet.

PlatformApplicationTransferResponse.title: Application Transfer
PlatformApplicationTransferResponse.description: Represents an application transfer request. Application transfers allow transferring ownership of an application from one organization to another.

id.description: The unique identifier for the application transfer.

code.description: A unique code for the transfer that can be shared with the recipient to claim the application through the clerk dashboard: https://dashboard.clerk.com/apps/transfer?code=<CODE>

application_id.description: The ID of the application being transferred.

status.description: The current status of the transfer.

expires_at.description: The timestamp when the transfer expires if not completed.

created_at.description: The timestamp when the transfer was created.

canceled_at.description: The timestamp when the transfer was canceled, or null if not canceled.

completed_at.description: The timestamp when the transfer was completed, or null if not completed.

PlatformListApplicationTransfersResponse.title: List Application Transfers Response
PlatformListApplicationTransfersResponse.description: A paginated list of application transfers.

total_count.description: The total number of application transfers matching the query.

InstanceUsageMeterWithBillable.title: Instance Usage Meter With Billable
InstanceUsageMeterWithBillable.description: Usage totals for a meter that includes both total and billable quantities.

total_usage.description: Total quantity for this meter in the selected period.

total_billable_usage.description: Billable subset of total usage for this meter in the selected period.

InstanceUsageMeter.title: Instance Usage Meter
InstanceUsageMeter.description: Usage totals for a single meter.

total_usage.description: Total quantity for this meter in the selected period.

InstanceSMSUsage.title: Instance SMS Usage
InstanceSMSUsage.description: Total SMS usage with per-geographic-tier breakdowns.

total_usage.description: Total SMS messages across all tiers.

PlatformInstanceUsageResponse.title: Instance Usage Report
PlatformInstanceUsageResponse.description: Usage report for an application instance containing per-meter totals.

period_start.description:
Inclusive start of the usage period boundary (RFC3339). When an
explicit date range is provided, this is midnight UTC on the start
date. When no date range is provided, this is the start of the
current billing cycle.

period_end.description:
Exclusive end of the usage period boundary (RFC3339). When an
explicit date range is provided, this is midnight UTC immediately
after the requested end date. When no date range is provided, this
is the end of the current billing cycle.

object.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same value.

matches_sso_connection.description: Indicates whether this email address domain matches an active enterprise connection.

object.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same value.
String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same value.
String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same value.

last_used_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the passkey was last used.

object.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same value.

provider_user_id.description: The unique ID of the user in the external provider's system

email_address_verified.description: Whether the email was verified by the OAuth provider at creation time. null = unknown (pre-migration data or custom OAuth providers), true = provider confirmed email was verified, false = provider confirmed email was NOT verified

object.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same value.
String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same value.

protocol.description: The authentication protocol used to sign in.

provider_user_id.description: The unique ID of the user in the external provider's system

role_set_key.description: The key of the [role set](https://clerk.com/docs/guides/organizations/control-access/role-sets) assigned to this organization.

OrganizationMembershipPublicUserData.description: An organization membership with public user data populated

OrganizationMembership.description: A user's membership in an organization

object.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same value.

public_metadata.description: Metadata saved on the organization membership, accessible from both Frontend and Backend APIs

private_metadata.description: Metadata saved on the organization membership, accessible only from the Backend API

object.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same value.

mfa_enabled_at.description: Unix timestamp of when MFA was last enabled for this user. It should be noted that this field is not nullified if MFA is disabled.

mfa_disabled_at.description: Unix timestamp of when MFA was last disabled for this user. It should be noted that this field is not nullified if MFA is enabled again.

password_last_updated_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the user's password was last updated.

banned.description: Flag to denote whether user is banned or not.

locked.description: Flag to denote whether user is currently locked, i.e. restricted from signing in or not.

lockout_expires_in_seconds.description: The number of seconds remaining until the lockout period expires for a locked user. A null value for a locked user indicates that lockout never expires.

verification_attempts_remaining.description: The number of verification attempts remaining until the user is locked. Null if account lockout is not enabled. Note: if a user is locked explicitly via the Backend API, they may still have verification attempts remaining.

delete_self_enabled.description: If enabled, user can delete themselves via FAPI.

create_organization_enabled.description: If enabled, user can create organizations via FAPI.

create_organizations_limit.description: The maximum number of organizations the user can create. 0 means unlimited.

last_active_at.description: Unix timestamp of the latest session activity, with day precision.

legal_accepted_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the user accepted the legal requirements.

bypass_client_trust.description: When set to `true`, the user will bypass client trust checks during sign-in.

PlatformListInstanceUsersResponse.title: List Instance Users Response
PlatformListInstanceUsersResponse.description: A paginated list of users for an application instance.

total_count.description: The total number of users in the instance.

PlatformListRedirectURLsResponse.title: List Redirect URLs Response
PlatformListRedirectURLsResponse.description: A paginated list of redirect URLs for an application instance.

total_count.description: The total number of redirect URLs in the instance.

PlatformConfigResponse.description: Instance configuration response containing key-value pairs and metadata.

config_version.description: Configuration version for optimistic concurrency control.

PlatformConfigPatchRequest.description: Configuration updates to apply. Keys should match config key names.

PlatformConfigPatchResponse.description: Configuration patch response containing the result of the operation.

dry_run.description: Whether this was a dry run (changes were not applied).

before.description: Configuration state before the patch was applied.

after.description: Configuration state after the patch was applied.

PlatformConfigSchemaResponse.description: JSON Schema describing the available configuration options.

properties.description: Schema definitions for each configuration key.
